_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the L1 baseline infantryman and produce the L2 'Outfitted Levy' variant (Tier 1 mid).
_meta.input_image: UnitProgression/Infantry/Refs/L1_Base.png (1024x1024 native, downscaled from a 2048x2048 source — backup at Refs/L1_Base_2K.png; LOCKED 2026-05-06)
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — Outfitted Levy beat = 4 visible adds (quilted leather under-tunic short sleeves, iron rim rivets on shield, wider leather waist belt replacing rope cord, hip pouch on anatomical-RIGHT hip). Mirrors Archer L1->L2 prompt skeleton with Infantry-specific anchors (mail coif, mustard+red split livery, spiked wooden mace, round wooden shield with steel boss, full short-trimmed dark-brown beard). Single-input pass from Refs/L1_Base.png since L2 is the chain origin (no prior composite exists yet).
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.

v4 (2026-05-07): reduced shield rim rivets from 8 (ring) to 4 (cardinal points) per AD detail-economy — 8 small dots in a ring conflicted with the locked 'embossed studs ... use clean wider band instead' first-cut item; 4 cardinal-point rivets read as functional kit without the 'too many nits' pattern at thumbnail. The L4 continuous iron rim band still cleanly supersedes the L2-L3 cardinal-point rivets.

v5 (2026-05-07): dropped IRON RIM RIVETS positive add entirely — 4-cardinal-point rivets were bleeding through to other surfaces (helmet brow band, etc) during generation. The L4 continuous iron rim band still introduces iron-on-shield, just one level later. L2 adds reduced from 4 to 3.

v6 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27.

adds.title: QUILTED BROWN LEATHER UNDER-TUNIC SHORT SLEEVES
adds.summary: add short sleeves to the previously sleeveless tunic, emerging from under the existing mustard-yellow + crimson-red split livery tunic at the shoulder seam and covering each upper arm from the shoulder down to mid-bicep.

adds.title: WIDER BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT REPLACING THE ROPE CORD
adds.summary: replace the existing twisted-rope cord belt with a single wider warm-brown leather belt at the natural waist, slightly thicker than the rope cord was.

adds.title: HIP POUCH ON THE ANATOMICAL-RIGHT HIP
adds.summary: add a small rectangular brown leather pouch with a flap closure hanging from the new leather belt on his anatomical-RIGHT hip (the viewer-LEFT side of the belt line).

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Infantry/Refs/L1_Base.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: EDIT GOAL — Apply THREE visible additive upgrades to the infantryman in the input image, matching the warm-brown leather + dark-iron palette of his existing kit. The three upgrades together represent an 'Outfitted Levy' rank step — the conscript footman has finished outfitting his basic levy kit. (1) QUILTED BROWN LEATHER UNDER-TUNIC SHORT SLEEVES — add short sleeves to the previously sleeveless tunic, emerging from under the existing mustard-yellow + crimson-red split livery tunic at the shoulder seam and covering each upper arm from the shoulder down to mid-bicep. The sleeves are matte warm-brown leather with a soft quilted cross-stitching pattern (visible diagonal stitching grid), NOT studded, NOT armored, NOT metal. The existing brown leather forearm bracers stay where they are; there is now a roughly 1-inch gap of bare warm peachy-tan skin visible between the lower edge of the new short sleeve and the upper edge of each leather bracer. Both arms are sleeved symmetrically. (2) WIDER BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT REPLACING THE ROPE CORD — replace the existing twisted-rope cord belt with a single wider warm-brown leather belt at the natural waist, slightly thicker than the rope cord was. The new belt has a square dark-iron buckle centered at the front and visible edge stitching along the top and bottom edges. The rope cord is FULLY REMOVED — no twisted rope visible anywhere. (3) HIP POUCH ON THE ANATOMICAL-RIGHT HIP — add a small rectangular brown leather pouch with a flap closure hanging from the new leather belt on his anatomical-RIGHT hip (the viewer-LEFT side of the belt line). The pouch is about three inches tall and two inches wide, matte warm-brown leather with a clearly visible flap top secured by a small brown leather strap and a tiny brass stud. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: his chiseled chibi jawline; his stern flat closed-mouth expression with no smile and no snarl; his thick dark-brown eyebrows with a slight stern furrow; his large warm steel-blue eyes with a single bright catchlight in each iris and visible whites of the eyes; his prominent slightly-hooked nose; his warm peachy-tan skin tone with a soft rosy blush on the cheeks above the beard line; his FULL SHORT-TRIMMED DARK-BROWN BEARD covering the chin and lower cheeks reaching the upper neck — trimmed short on the cheeks, NOT bushy, NOT wild, NOT long; his dark mustache continuous with the beard, covering the upper lip but not extending past the corners of the mouth; his dark-brown short hair visible only as a thin fringe at the forehead under the front edge of the mail coif; the chunky scale-mail coif covering the entire scalp, ears, and neck down to the collarbone, with chunky overlapping rounded scale-shaped plates in cool silver-gray steel arranged in horizontal rows where each row overlaps the row below by half a scale, painted with a soft top-down highlight on each scale and a warm-brown shadow under the lower edge of each scale, draping around the shoulders to form a broad cowl-like collar and framing the face in an open oval; the sleeveless mustard-yellow and crimson-red split LIVERY TUNIC — viewer-LEFT half of the tunic chest is solid crimson-red, viewer-RIGHT half is mustard-yellow with a vertical crimson-red center stripe running collar to hem, simple round neckline, hem reaches mid-thigh — this red+mustard split livery tunic remains visibly DOMINANT on the chest, fully visible, NOT covered, NOT shrunk; the existing brown leather forearm bracers wrapping wrist to mid-forearm on both arms with a single visible cross-strap binding per bracer, identical on both arms; both bare hands warm peachy-tan skin (the anatomical-RIGHT hand grips the upper haft of the spiked mace just below the mace head; the anatomical-LEFT hand and forearm are concealed BEHIND the round shield); the spiked wooden mace held in the anatomical-RIGHT hand (viewer-LEFT side of frame) — heavy warm-brown wooden haft, bulbous wooden mace head at the striking end painted slightly darker brown than the haft, six visible iron spikes (pyramidal cool-gray steel cones) protruding radially from the mace head with a single spike on the top apex, mace head dangling at hip height angled down toward the viewer-LEFT, hand at the level of the head/upper coif gripping the haft just below the head; the round wooden buckler-style shield held in the anatomical-LEFT arm (viewer-RIGHT side of frame) — approximately 24 inches in diameter, warm-brown wooden plank face with faint vertical wood grain, central steel boss as a small cool-gray steel dome roughly 3 inches diameter, plain rim, NO rivets, NO studs, NO embossed dots, NO painted heraldry; the dark blue / muted steel-blue knee-length trousers slightly loose at the cuff with soft painted vertical fold lines; the chunky warm-brown leather boots reaching mid-calf with a darker folded ankle cuff at the top and wide flat soles. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left so the character's anatomical right side faces camera, weight planted evenly on both feet with a very slight forward lean. THE MACE ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, on the viewer-LEFT side of the frame) is extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, elbow bent at roughly 120 degrees. The hand grips the upper haft of the spiked wooden mace just below the bulbous mace head; the mace head hangs at hip height angled down toward the viewer-LEFT; the knuckles of the gripping hand are visible to camera. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, on the viewer-RIGHT side of the frame) is bent at the elbow, with the forearm angled across the body bringing the round wooden shield up to upper-chest / shoulder height. The shield's flat face presents toward the viewer at a slight three-quarter angle, NOT perfectly flat to camera, NOT edge-on. The hand and forearm are concealed BEHIND the shield. The pose reads as a calm guard stance: mace ready but not raised, shield up but not braced for impact. The pose is NOT a swing, NOT a charge, NOT a block in motion — both arms are static and balanced. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire character in deep warm brown-black with even weight, slight thickening in shadowed recesses (under the chin, under the coif drape, behind the shield rim), slight thinning where forms catch the key light. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match the input image exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 66.8% of canvas vertical height (~684px tall) and approximately 60.8% of canvas horizontal width (~623px wide, including the mace and shield extending the silhouette envelope). TOP of mail coif at approximately 16.6% from top of canvas (~170px headroom). BOTTOM of boots at approximately 16.6% from bottom of canvas (~170px below). Character vertically centered, NOT bottom-anchored. Approximately 18.3% empty margin viewer-LEFT, approximately 20.9% empty margin viewer-RIGHT. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop the head or feet, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. Clean white background with no environmental context, no ground plane, no scenery, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT remove or shrink or cover the mustard-yellow and crimson-red split livery tunic — the red+mustard chest livery must remain visibly DOMINANT on the chest, fully visible, identical in panel layout to the input image; DO NOT replace the tunic with a body coat, gambeson, surcoat, or armor; DO NOT cover the tunic chest with scale mail, chain mail, or any metal armor at this level; DO NOT add scale mail, chain mail, breastplate, cuirass, or any body metal armor — the only metal at is the existing mail coif and the existing central steel boss on the shield; DO NOT add a helm, steel cap, kettle helm, sallet, or any structural addition to the head — the head covering remains exactly the existing mail coif; DO NOT remove the mail coif or shrink it or replace the scale plates with chainmail rings or fine ring mesh — the coif stays as chunky overlapping scales identical to the input image; DO NOT change any facial feature including the jawline, brow shape, eye color, eye shape, nose shape, mouth shape, mustache shape, beard shape, beard length, or skin tone; DO NOT grow the beard into a wild bushy beard or extend it past the upper neck; DO NOT remove the beard or trim it to clean-shaven; DO NOT change the hair color or hair fringe under the coif; DO NOT change the livery tunic colors, panel layout, neckline, or hem; DO NOT change the trouser color (dark blue / muted steel-blue) or boot color (warm brown); DO NOT add metal sabatons, greaves, leg armor, or any leg metal at this level (those arrive at this level); DO NOT swap the hand holding the mace; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the mace shape, the wooden mace head shape, the iron spike count, the iron spike arrangement, or replace the wooden head with a flanged steel head (steel flanges arrive at this level); DO NOT add iron banding to the mace head or wrap the haft with leather grip cord (those arrive at this level / respectively); DO NOT change the round shield to a heater shield, kite shield, tower shield, square shield, or any non-round shape — the shield stays round; DO NOT add cross-strap iron bands radiating from the boss (those arrive at this level); DO NOT add a continuous iron rim band around the shield perimeter (that arrives at this level); DO NOT add iron rivets, studs, embossed dots, or any small metal punctuation along the shield rim, the wooden shield face, the leather belt, the leather sleeves, the bracers, the mail coif, the boots, or anywhere else on the figure; DO NOT add metal banding rings, brow bands with rivets, or any riveted hardware appearing as decoration on the helmet, coif, or shield; DO NOT add painted heraldry, emblems, or coats of arms to the shield face; DO NOT add a knife sheath or second hip pouch — exactly ONE pouch at the anatomical-RIGHT hip at this level; DO NOT add a pouch on the anatomical-LEFT hip; DO NOT keep any twisted-rope cord at the waist — the rope is FULLY REPLACED by the new wider leather belt; DO NOT add a leather X-strap chest harness, baldric, sword belt, or any chest strap at this level; DO NOT add steel pauldrons, shoulder caps, or any shoulder armor at this level (a single pauldron arrives at this level); DO NOT add gold trim, gold buckle, gold accents, gold rivets, gold band, or any gold anywhere; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, engraved patterns, or filigree; DO NOT make either bracer studded, vambrace-shaped, metal, or armored — both stay matte plain warm-brown leather with the existing single cross-strap bindings only; DO NOT make the new short sleeves studded, plated, scale, or armored — they stay matte quilted warm-brown leather only; DO NOT change the chibi proportions or head-to-body ratio; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, add a ground plane, or add cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white — the background must be clean opaque white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
